summary: Canadian short story writer, winner of the 2013 Nobel Prize in Literature. Most commonly identified by 'Dance of the Happy Shades' (Miss Marsalles's piano recital, disabled student plays Gluck), 'The Moons of Jupiter' (Janet, planetarium, dying father), 'The Bear Came Over the Mountain' (Grant, Fiona, Alzheimer's), and 'Boys and Girls' (narrator dismissed as 'only a girl'). Collections include Dear Life, Runaway, Hateship Friendship Courtship Loveship Marriage, and Lives of Girls and Women.

works.name: The Moons of Jupiter
works.description: The most frequently clued Munro story. Janet visits a planetarium between visits to her hospitalized father, who is awaiting heart surgery. She reflects on her strained relationship with her estranged daughter Nichola and the leukemia scare that may have altered her love. Her father remembers the phrase 'shoreless seas' from a Joaquin Miller poem.

clues.clue: Janet visits a planetarium between visits to her hospitalized/dying father
examples: Janet goes to a planetarium between visits to her hospitalized father
Between visits to her father in the hospital, Janet watches a show at the planetarium

clues.clue: Janet's strained relationship with estranged daughter Nichola; leukemia scare altered her love
examples: The protagonist of that story by this author notes how her daughter grew up 'incommunicado' after a leukemia scare
the narrator worries that a leukemia scare altered her love for her daughter Nichola

clues.clue: Father remembers 'shoreless seas' from Joaquin Miller poem as daughter enters room
examples: a man remembers the line 'shoreless seas' from a Joaquin Miller poem as his daughter enters the room
her father says 'in a surprised voice, Shore-less seas' as he begins to quote from Joaquin Miller's poem 'Columbus'

works.name: Dance of the Happy Shades
works.description: The title story of Munro's first collection. At Miss Marsalles's annual piano recital, a girl with Down syndrome (or other disability) from the Greenhill School plays the title Gluck piece (from Orfeo ed Euridice) beautifully, astonishing the suburban parents who had pitied Miss Marsalles.

clues.clue: Miss Marsalles hosts piano recital; disabled girl from Greenhill School plays the title Gluck piece beautifully
examples: a girl with Down syndrome plays the title piece
a disabled girl from Greenhill School who plays a beautiful rendition of the title piece at Miss Marsalles's piano recital

clues.clue: Dolores Boyes plays excerpt from Gluck's Orfeo ed Euridice
examples: Dolores Boyes plays an excerpt from Gluck's Orfeo ed Euridice at a piano recital

works.name: The Bear Came Over the Mountain
works.description: Grant's wife Fiona develops Alzheimer's and is moved to the Meadowlake nursing home, where she falls in love with another resident, Aubrey. Grant reluctantly seduces Aubrey's wife Marian to arrange for Aubrey to stay at the nursing home with Fiona. Fiona reads about Iceland to connect to her heritage. Adapted into the film Away from Her.

clues.clue: Grant's wife Fiona develops Alzheimer's; falls in love with another man (Aubrey) at nursing home
examples: A retired professor loses his Alzheimer's-stricken wife Fiona to another man
Grant reluctantly seduces Marian so that his dementia-afflicted wife can have Marian's husband, Aubrey

clues.clue: Grant seduces Aubrey's wife Marian so Aubrey can return to nursing home with Fiona
examples: a former professor of Icelandic legends attempts to kindle a romance with a woman so that her husband, Aubrey, can return to a nursing home with the Alzheimer's-stricken Fiona

clues.clue: Fiona reads Letters from Iceland; Grant brings her a book about Iceland; connects to Icelandic heritage
examples: Fiona recalls reading Letters from Iceland to try to connect to her Icelandic heritage
Grant brings her another book about Iceland after her battle with Alzheimer's makes her forget that she is married

clues.clue: Grant tells Fiona 'Not a chance' when she says he could have 'forsook' or 'forsooken' her
examples: Grant replies, 'Not a chance,' when Fiona tells him that he could have 'forsook' or 'forsooken' her

works.name: Boys and Girls
works.description: The unnamed tomboy narrator lives on a fox farm and impulsively opens a gate to let the horse Flora escape before her father can shoot it. Her father dismisses her as 'only a girl,' marking her gendered coming-of-age.

clues.clue: Narrator lets horse Flora escape; father dismisses her as 'only a girl'
examples: a girl tries to free the horse Flora before her fox-farming father can shoot it
Laird's older sister lets a horse escape slaughter and is dismissed with the phrase, 'She's only a girl'

clues.clue: Set on a fox farm; father is a fox farmer
examples: this author's fox-farming father dismisses her as 'only a girl' after she opens a gate for the horse Flora

works.name: Hateship, Friendship, Courtship, Loveship, Marriage
works.description: Two girls, Edith and Sabitha, forge love letters supposedly from Sabitha's father Ken to the housekeeper Johanna. Johanna ships furniture to her future husband, cures him of bronchitis, and eventually marries him. Ends with a girl translating a Latin passage about 'what fate has in store.'

clues.clue: Sabitha and Edith forge love letters from father to housekeeper Johanna; Johanna ships furniture and marries him
examples: This author wrote about Sabitha forging love letters supposedly from her father to her housekeeper Johanna, who later marries him
Johanna ships furniture to her future husband, then cures him of his bronchitis, after being tricked by two girls writing fake love letters

clues.clue: Ends with girl translating Latin passage about 'what fate has in store' while thinking about baby Omar
examples: a girl translates a Latin passage about 'what fate has in store' while thinking about a baby named Omar

works.name: The Love of a Good Woman
works.description: Three boys about to swim in a river find the dead optometrist Mr. Willens in a submerged car. Enid cares for Mrs. Quinn, who recounts the murder. A character witnesses her father's affair and thinks his mistress's breast is an ice cream cone.

clues.clue: Three boys find dead optometrist Mr. Willens in submerged car; Enid cares for Mrs. Quinn
examples: three boys about to swim in a river find a dead optometrist in a submerged car
That character created by this author cares for Mrs. Quinn, who recounts the murder of the optometrist Mr. Willens

clues.clue: Character witnesses father's affair; thinks mistress's breast is an ice cream cone
examples: One character created by this author witnesses her father's affair and thinks his mistress' breast is an ice cream cone

works.name: General / Other Stories
works.description: Additional frequently mentioned stories include 'Too Much Happiness' (Sofia Kovalevskaya), 'The Peace of Utrecht' (Helen breaks deal, Maddy cares for mother with Parkinson's), 'Miles City, Montana' (near-drowning of daughter, funeral for drowned boy Steve Gauley), 'Free Radicals' (widow tricks murderer), 'Chance' (Juliet snubs stranger on train who then commits suicide), 'Something I've Been Meaning to Tell You' (Char swallows laundry blueing, Et drives her to suicide), 'Runaway' (Carla's abusive husband kills pet goat), Lives of Girls and Women (Del Jordan's maturation), Dear Life, and 'Family Furnishings.' Won 2013 Nobel Prize.

clues.clue: Canadian author; won 2013 Nobel Prize in Literature
examples: what Canadian short story writer?
the Canadian winner of the 2013 Nobel in Literature

clues.clue: Lives of Girls and Women: coming-of-age story cycle about Del Jordan
examples: this author chronicled the maturation of Del Jordan in the story cycle Lives of Girls and Women

clues.clue: 'The Peace of Utrecht': Helen breaks deal to return from college; Maddy cares for mother with Parkinson's
examples: a deal to return from college after four years is broken by Helen, forcing Maddy to look after their mother, who has Parkinson's disease

clues.clue: 'Miles City, Montana': near-drowning of daughter in swimming pool; narrator recalls funeral of drowned Steve Gauley
examples: the near-drowning of the narrator's daughter in a swimming pool causes the narrator to recall the funeral held in her house for a drowned eight-year-old boy

clues.clue: 'Chance': Juliet snubs stranger on train who then commits suicide
examples: Juliet snubs a stranger in one of these places, who then commits suicide

clues.clue: 'The Progress of Love': narrator falsely claims father watched mother burn 3,000-dollar inheritance
examples: the narrator falsely claims to Bob Marks that her father stood by and watched as her mother burned a 3,000-dollar inheritance in the kitchen stove

clues.clue: Pedro Almodovar adapted Juliet stories as the film Julieta
examples: His most recent film is a Spanish-language adaptation of Munro's Juliet stories called Julieta

comprehensive_summary:
Alice Munro is one of the most frequently clued short story writers in quizbowl, consistently identifiable through a handful of signature stories. 'The Moons of Jupiter' is her most heavily clued work, appearing in nearly every tossup: Janet visits a planetarium between visits to her dying father in the hospital, reflects on her estranged daughter Nichola and a leukemia scare, and her father remembers the phrase 'shoreless seas' from a Joaquin Miller poem. 'Dance of the Happy Shades' is the second most common giveaway, always identified through the piano recital at Miss Marsalles's house where a disabled girl from the Greenhill School plays the title Gluck piece beautifully.

'The Bear Came Over the Mountain' receives extensive treatment as a standalone bonus and tossup subject. Grant's wife Fiona develops Alzheimer's and falls in love with Aubrey at a nursing home; Grant seduces Aubrey's wife Marian to ensure Fiona can stay with Aubrey. Fiona's connection to her Icelandic heritage through reading Letters from Iceland is a recurring power clue. 'Boys and Girls,' the story of a tomboy on a fox farm who lets the horse Flora escape and is dismissed as 'only a girl,' is a standard mid-level clue.

'Hateship, Friendship, Courtship, Loveship, Marriage' appears regularly, identified through the forged love letters that Sabitha and Edith write from Sabitha's father to the housekeeper Johanna. 'The Love of a Good Woman' features the boys finding the dead optometrist Mr. Willens in a submerged car. Less common stories include 'Too Much Happiness' (Sofia Kovalevskaya), 'Miles City, Montana,' 'Free Radicals,' 'Chance,' and 'The Progress of Love.'

Munro is always identified as Canadian and as the 2013 Nobel laureate. Her collections Lives of Girls and Women, Runaway, Dear Life, and The View from Castle Rock appear as giveaway identifiers. She is frequently linked to Margaret Atwood and other Canadian authors.
